zh.lifeSummary: 當Lewis Land於1759年出生在美國維吉尼亞州時，他的父親Bird Land22歲，母親Rebekah Lewellin16歲。他於大約1805年與Elizabeth Martha Ray在美國南卡羅來納州結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1854年7月28日在美國南卡羅來納州格林維爾享年95歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lewis Land于1759年出生在美国弗吉尼亚州时，他的父亲Bird Land22岁，母亲Rebekah Lewellin16岁。他于大约1805年与Elizabeth Martha Ray在美国南卡罗来纳州结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他去世于1854年7月28日在美国南卡罗来纳州格林維爾享年95岁。

nameMeanings.name: Land
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, German, and Dutch: topographic name from Old English, Middle Dutch land, Middle High German lant ‘land, territory’. This had more specialized senses in the Middle Ages, being used to denote the countryside as opposed to a town or an estate.
English: topographic name from Middle English launde ‘glade’ (Old French land), or a habitational name from a place called with this word, such as Launde in Leicestershire or Laund in Yorkshire.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of the three farmsteads so named, from Old Norse land ‘land, territory’ (see 1 above).

variants.name: Lander
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): topographic or status name from Middle High German lant, German Land ‘land, territory’ (see Land 1). Compare Landmann .
German: topographic name from Middle High German lander ‘picket fence’.
German: from the personal name Lanther, see Landherr 1.

variants.name: Lands
meanings: English: variant of   Land  .

variants.name: Landt
meanings: German: variant of   Land   1.

variants.name: Lande
meanings: Norwegian: habitational name from any of several farmsteads, so named from either the dative singular or the plural form of land (see Land 3).
German: from the old personal name Lando, a short form of ancient Germanic names formed with lant ‘land’ (see Land ).

French: topographic name for someone living on a heath, moor, French lande (from Gaulish landa ‘space, land’), or a habitational name from any of numerous minor places called La Lande from this word. Compare Lalande .

variants.name: Landfried
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Chantel.
South German: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements land ‘land, territory’ (see Land ) + fred, frid ‘peace’. This surname is also found in France (Lorraine).

variants.name: Lawn
meanings: English:
variant of   Land   2.
from Middle English laun ‘lawn, fine linen’ (French laun, from the name of the city of Laon), perhaps for a maker or trader.

variants.name: Landa
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Jorge, Jose, Luis, Juan, Manuel, Enrique, Jesus, Julio, Mario, Roberto, Miguel.

Basque: topographic name from landa ‘(large) field, prairie’. There is a place of this name in Araba/Álava province, Basque Country (Spain), and the surname may also be a habitational name from this place.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of several farmsteads in southwestern Norway, so named from the plural of land ‘land, territory’ (see Land 3).

variants.name: Lant
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Land   with devoicing of the final consonant.

nameMeanings.name: Lewis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form, since the Middle Ages, of the French name Louis . In modern use it is also in part a transferred use of the surname derived from this given name, or from Anglicized forms of Gaelic Mac Lughaidh ‘son of Lughaidh ’ or Welsh Llewelyn .

variants.name: Lewys
meanings: form, since the Middle Ages, of the French name Louis . In modern use it is also in part a transferred use of the surname derived from this given name, or from Anglicized forms of Gaelic Mac Lughaidh ‘son of Lughaidh ’ or Welsh Llewelyn .

historicEvents.description: Thomas Jefferson's American Declaration of Independence endorsed by Congress. Colonies declare independence.

historicEvents.title: Richmond Becomes the Capital
historicEvents.description: On April 18, 1780 Richmond became the capital of Virginia. It was the temporary capital from 1780-1788.
historicEvents.urlTitle: United States History- History of Richmond Virginia

historicEvents.title: Shays' Rebellion
historicEvents.description: Caused by war veteran Daniel Shays, Shays' Rebellion was to protest economic and civil rights injustices that he and other farmers were seeing after the Revolutionary War. Because of the Rebellion it opened the eyes of the governing officials that the Articles of Confederation needed a reform. The Rebellion served as a guardrail when helping reform the United States Constitution.

historicEvents.title: Becomes the 10th state
historicEvents.description: On June 25, 1788 Virginia became the 10th state.

historicEvents.title: Monumental Church Built
historicEvents.description: The Monumental Church was built between 1812-1814 on the sight where the Richmond Theatre fire had taken place. It is a monument to those that died in the fire.

historicEvents.title: The Black Hawk War
historicEvents.description: Convinced that a group of Native American tribes were hostile, The United States formed a frontier militia to stop them in their tracks. Even though Black Hawk was hoping to avoid bloodshed while trying to resettle on tribal land, U.S. officials opened fire on the Native Americans. Black Hawk then responded to this confrontation by successfully attacking the militia at the Battle of Stillman's Run and then left northward. After a few months the militia caught up with Black Hawk and his men and defeated them at the Battle of Wisconsin Heights. While being weakened by hunger, injuries and desertion, Black Hawk and the rest of the many native survivors retreated towards the Mississippi. Unfortunately, Black Hawk and other leaders were later captured when they surrendered to the US forces and were then imprisoned for a year.

historicEvents.title: Lumpkin's Jail
historicEvents.description: In 1844 when Robert Lumpkin bought land in Virginia, this would be the spot of the Infamous Slave Jail (or Lumpkin’s Jail). The slaves would be brought here during the slave trade until they were sold. Lumpkin had purchased the land for his own slave business.

en.lifeSummary: When Lewis Land was born in 1759, in Virginia, United States, his father, Bird Land, was 22 and his mother, Rebekah Lewellin, was 16. He married Elizabeth Martha Ray about 1805, in South Carolina, United States. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 5 daughters. He died on 28 July 1854, in Greenville, South Carolina, United States, at the age of 95.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lewis Land est né en 1759, à Virginie, États-Unis, son père, Bird Land, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Rebekah Lewellin, avait 16 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Martha Ray environ 1805, à Caroline du Sud, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 5 filles. Il est décédé le 28 juillet 1854, à Greenville, Caroline du Sud, États-Unis, à l’âge de 95 ans.

Er hat um 1805 in South Carolina, Vereinigte Staaten Elizabeth Martha Ray geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Er starb am 28. Juli 1854 im Alter von 95 Jahren in Greenville, South Carolina, Vereinigte Staaten.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lewis Land è nato il nel 1759 nel Virginia, Stati Uniti d'America, il suo padre, Bird Land, aveva 22 anni e il suo madre, Rebekah Lewellin, aveva 16 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth Martha Ray circa 1805, nel Carolina del Sud, Stati Uniti d'America. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 5 figlie. Morì il 28 luglio 1854, nel Greenville, Carolina del Sud, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 95 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Lewis Landが1759年にアメリカ合衆国バージニア州で生まれた時，父Bird Landは22歳，母Rebekah Lewellinは16歳であった。約1805年にアメリカ合衆国サウスカロライナ州でElizabeth Martha Rayと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼は1854年7月28日にアメリカ合衆国サウスカロライナ州グレエンヴィルで95歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lewis Land(이)가 1759년 미국 버지니아 주에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Bird Land(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Rebekah Lewellin(은)는 16세였다. 그는 대략 1805년 미국 사우스캐롤라이나 주에서 Elizabeth Martha Ray(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1854년 7월 28일 미국 사우스캐롤라이나 주 그린빌에서 95세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lewis Land родился в 1759 г. в местности Вирджиния, Соединенные штаты, его отцу по имени Bird Land было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Rebekah Lewellin, 16. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Martha Ray около 1805 г. в местности Южная Каролина, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Он умер 28 июля 1854 г. в местности гринвилл, Южная Каролина, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 95 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lewis Land nació en 1759, en Virginia, Estados Unidos, su papá, Bird Land, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Rebekah Lewellin, tenía 16 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Martha Ray aproximadamente 1805, en Carolina del Sur, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 28 de julio de 1854, en Greenville, Carolina del Sur, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 95 años.
